purpose: Represent video action model architectures, training recipes, data roles, runtime paths, and diagram primitives in a single comparable structure.

sourcePolicy.normalizationNote: Fields are normalized for comparison. They are not direct quotations unless explicitly marked elsewhere.
uncertaintyLevels: low: direct paper text or survey table support
medium: synthesized from survey notes and extracted paper text
high: survey-only or missing local full paper

modelFields.name: Short display name.
modelFields.title: Full paper title.
modelFields.localText: Local extracted text filename when available.
modelFields.family: One of pixel_idm, latent_idm, encoder_only, joint_latent, unified, multi_stream, implicit_future, latent_action, alignment, multimodal, online_adaptation, speedup.
modelFields.problem: Primary problem the paper exists to solve.

insights.novelty: What is new compared with nearby models.

metrics.runtimeCost: 1 fast action-only, 5 very slow full video rollout.
metrics.computeScale: 1 small/adapter scale, 5 foundation-scale or heavy generation.
metrics.evidence: 1 early or survey-only, 5 strong multi-benchmark/real-world evidence.

diagram.data: Training data sources and their role.

predictiveState.form: pixels, latent features, latent actions, RGB-D, tactile state, condition vector, value trajectory, etc.
predictiveState.runtime: offline, none, partial, implicit, full, async, or online_adaptive.
predictiveState.rendered: Whether future pixels are decoded at runtime.

diagram.trainingStages: Named stages with data and objective.
diagram.runtimePath: Modules used on the deployed control path.
diagram.outputs: Action, future, value, depth, tactile, or synthetic-data outputs.

literalArchitecture.sourceExtract: Path to generated method extract markdown.
literalArchitecture.sourceLines: Line ranges in the extracted paper text that support the diagram.
literalArchitecture.inputTokens: Concrete input modalities/tokens, e.g. T5 language embeddings, VAE latent frames, proprioceptive MLP tokens, noisy action tokens.
literalArchitecture.tokenization: How inputs enter the model: VAE, VQ tokenizer, MLP projection, patchification, codebook, action chunk tokenization.
literalArchitecture.backbone: Named core model blocks with concrete architecture family and pretrained backbone where stated.
literalArchitecture.branches: Parallel or conditional branches: video branch, depth branch, action expert, tactile branch, value branch, future-prefix branch.
literalArchitecture.attention: Attention pattern, masking, cross-attention, information-flow restrictions, or stream fusion.
literalArchitecture.heads: Concrete prediction heads: action decoder, denoiser, depth decoder, IDM, value head, future latent head.
literalArchitecture.objectives: Losses and training objectives: flow matching, diffusion denoising, reconstruction, alignment, contrastive, RL/post-training rewards.
literalArchitecture.trainingRecipe: Stage-by-stage method details grounded in paper methods.
literalArchitecture.inferenceRecipe: Actual deployment computation, including what is removed, cached, partially denoised, or asynchronously scheduled.
literalArchitecture.diagramModules: Literal diagram blocks to render, each with id, lane, label, type, details, and optional source line reference.
literalArchitecture.diagramEdges: Directed links between modules with labels such as tokenizes, cross-attends, denoises, supervises, trains-only, runtime.

modelFields.uncertainty: Short note about source coverage and interpretation risk.

families.label: Pixel-space IDM
families.diagramThesis: Predict or synthesize visual futures, then infer actions from decoded or inspectable pixel-space video.

families.label: Latent-space IDM
families.diagramThesis: Infer actions from hidden video-model features or semantic future embeddings instead of decoded pixel-space rollout.

families.label: Training-time video, action-only runtime
families.diagramThesis: Use video prediction as representational pressure, then deploy a compact action decoder.

families.label: Shared action-observation latent space
families.diagramThesis: Put actions, proprioception, future state, or motion chains into a common predictive geometry.

families.label: Unified action-observation prediction
families.diagramThesis: Train action and future observation as coupled outputs of one temporal model.

families.label: Separate streams or expert mixtures
families.diagramThesis: Keep video, language, action, or modality experts separate while exchanging predictive state.

families.label: Implicit future representation
families.diagramThesis: Predict future embeddings, condition vectors, or value trajectories without rendering future video.

families.label: Latent action interface
families.diagramThesis: Learn a mid-level action code from video and ground it to executable robot commands.

families.label: Representation or executability alignment
families.diagramThesis: Improve an existing world/action model by aligning to physics, geometry, rewards, or executable actions.

families.label: Tactile, depth, or 4D state
families.diagramThesis: Inject physical state variables into the predictive backbone rather than only into the final action head.

families.label: Online adaptive world-action policy
families.diagramThesis: Use prediction error after deployment as self-supervision for small online updates.

families.label: Shortcut or deployment acceleration
families.diagramThesis: Distill, cache, or asynchronously schedule foresight so the action path stays fast.
